publication/title: Mal-Prec: computational prediction of protein Malonylation sites via machine learning based feature integration
publication/authors: Liu, X; Wang, L; Li, J; Hu, J; Zhang, X

dataset/redundancy:
* **Redundancy Methodology**: CD-HIT was employed to remove redundant sequences with equal to or more than 40% similarities. The processed sequences were truncated into 17-residue long sequence segments with lysine (K) located at the center.

* **Independent Sets**: Yes. The authors used a down-sampling method to construct a balanced dataset and split 20% of the dataset (695) as an independent dataset, with the remaining 80% (2775) as the training dataset.

* **Independence Enforcement**: The authors used a down-sampling method to construct a balanced dataset and split 20% of the dataset (695) as an independent dataset, with the remaining 80% (2775) as the training dataset.

optimization/algorithm:
* ML Algorithm Class:
  * Support Vector Machine (SVM).

* Algorithm Novelty: Application of existing method.
optimization/meta:
* Meta-predictor: Yes.

  * Random Forest (RF)
  * K-nearest neighbors (KNN)
  * Ensemble of decision tree
  * Naive Bayes (NB)
optimization/encoding:
* **Data Encoding Method: Sliding Window**
  * A sliding window of length 17 was used to select peptide segments with lysine at the center point.
  * The dataset was balanced by down-sampling to ensure equal quantities of positive and negative data sets.

* **Data Encoding Method: CKSAAP**
  * Composition of K-Spaced Amino Acid Pairs (CKSAAP) was used to represent peptide segments.

* **Data Encoding Method: AAindex**
  * Amino Acid Index Properties (AAindex) was used to represent peptide segments.

* **Data Encoding Method: One-Hot Encoding**
  * One-hot encoding was used to transform amino acids into orthogonal numeric vectors.
  * Each peptide sequence was represented as a 21-dimensional vector, accounting for 20 conventional amino acids and 1 pseudo amino acid (X).
optimization/parameters:
* **Parameter Count (p)**:
  * Not enough information is available.

* **Architecture Selection**:
  * Architecture Selection: The authors used grid search to optimize the two important parameters of SVM, c and g, which are the penalty parameters and kernel parameters respectively in the SVM algorithm. The radial basis function was adopted as the kernel function.
optimization/features:
* Number of Features (f): 1764
  * The CKSAAP feature vectors were 441*4 = 1764-dimensions.

* Training-Restricted Selection: Unclear
  * Not enough information is available to confirm if feature selection was restricted to the training set only.
optimization/fitting:
* **p > N: No**

  The training set size (N) is 1768 sequence fragments. The CKSAAP feature vectors were 1764-dimensions. The model complexity is not larger than the training set size.

* **Over-fitting Evidence**: 5-fold cross-validation

  The authors used 5-fold cross-validation to conduct model selection, which can effectively avoid over-learning and under-learning.

Regularization Techniques:

optimization/config: * Configuration Source: The model is coded in MATLAB and is publicly available at https://github.com/flyinsky6/Mal-Prec, together with the data sets used in this study | Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License.
model/interpretability:
* **Model Interpretability**: Black-box.
  * The model uses a Support Vector Machine (SVM) classifier, which is generally considered a black-box model due to its complex decision boundaries and kernel transformations.
* **XAI Methods**: None.
  * The paper does not mention the use of any specific Explainable AI (XAI) methods to interpret the predictions made by the Mal-Prec model.
model/output:
* Prediction Task: Classification.
* Target Variable: Malonylation site presence in protein sequences.
